title: COMMUNITY EDUCATION CLASSES AND EARLY-STAGE PROGRAMS - 211info
text: ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION OREGON COMMUNITY EDUCATION CLASSES AND EARLY-STAGE PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers education programs to help people understand what to expect with and how to prepare for having and caring for Alzheimer's and other dementias. Courses are offered online and in-person in local communities. 5285 Meadows Road Suite 451 Lake Oswego, OR 97035 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Support group times vary Email (800) 272-3900 247 , Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Helpline (800) 272-3900 24/7 support, information and resources Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: Helpline: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement On-site: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Services: Caregiver Training * Alzheimer's Disease Disease/Disability Information * Alzheimer's Disease Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - 24/7 HELPLINE MUTUAL SUPPORT - SUPPORT GROUPS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION OREGON
